steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Albert used an unconventional surface (concrete) for graffiti, and David has chosen an unusual canvas (ice floes) for charcoal art. The natural relationship between these two sentences is one of similarity or parallel.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
”Likewise” directly expresses that relationship of similarity—when two people make related unconventional choices, “likewise” connects them as parallel innovations.

The other three options either wrongly signal contrast (However), don't properly bridge from father to son (Indeed), or enumerate instead of parallel (Second).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
